text: I have barely gotten out of bed since my uncle died. Everything reminds me of him, and I cannot understand how life is supposed to continue.
My manager took credit for the work I stayed up all week finishing. I feel furious every time I think about it.
I keep checking my phone because the doctor still has not called. I know something may be wrong and I cannot focus on anything else.
I lied to my friend because I was embarrassed, and now I cannot stop thinking about how much I hurt her.
After months of worrying, the scan came back clear. I finally feel like I can breathe again.
I got the internship I wanted. I worked so hard for this and I am honestly proud of myself.
My partner remembered the tiny detail I mentioned weeks ago and surprised me with it. I feel so loved and grateful.
The instructions changed three times and nobody can explain what we are supposed to submit. I feel completely lost.
I thought the presentation had gone badly, but everyone started clapping. I did not expect that at all.
I want to apply, but I am scared I will fail and waste everyone's time. Part of me is excited, though.
Nothing terrible happened today. I made dinner, answered some emails, and went for a walk.
My sister is struggling and I keep thinking about what I can do to make things easier for her.
They ignored every safety rule and then acted surprised when someone got hurt. It is disgusting.
I studied for weeks and still failed by one point. I am not angry, just deeply disappointed.
I saw an unfamiliar light moving across the sky and spent the rest of the night trying to understand what it was.
I finally understood why that conversation bothered me so much: I felt dismissed, not merely disagreed with.
Everyone praised my design, but I keep thinking they will realize I am not actually talented.
The trip was canceled because of the storm. I know nobody caused it, but I am still upset and helpless.
She defended me when nobody else would. I admire her courage more than I can explain.
I am irritated that the meeting ran late, but it is not a big deal and I will probably forget tomorrow.
I cannot tell whether I am sad that it ended or relieved that I no longer have to keep trying.
The result is out tomorrow. I have done everything I can, so now I am trying to accept whatever happens.
I volunteered to lead the project and it succeeded, but I also know the team carried me through the difficult parts.
I read the message several times. It was not openly rude, yet something about it made me uncomfortable.
